content:
Case 9:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 81
- Sex: male
- Chief complaint: New-onset swelling of the right shoulder and upper arm with localized pain and reduced range of motion
- Vital signs: BP 118/70 mmHg, HR 92/min (irregular), Temp 36.9 °C, RR 18/min, SpO₂ 95 % (room air)
Setting: Outpatient clinic (initial presentation with localized musculoskeletal swelling)
Capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically stable patient in an ambulatory hematology setting. You can obtain a detailed history and examination, request routine blood work, and arrange standard non-invasive imaging appropriate for an initial assessment. Invasive biopsy procedures and complex diagnostic evaluations require additional planning.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "physical examination"}}

History Physical:
2025-07-03: The patient presented to the hematology outpatient clinic with new-onset swelling of the right shoulder and upper arm, which developed over the past several weeks and was associated with localized pain and reduced range of motion. He also reported foamy urine, but denied constitutional B symptoms such as fever, night sweats, or unintentional weight loss. There was no recent history of infection or trauma. Physical examination revealed asymmetric swelling of the right shoulder and upper arm with mild tenderness to palpation. No overlying erythema or warmth was noted. No palpable cervical, axillary, or inguinal lymphadenopathy. Lungs were clear on auscultation; cardiac rhythm was irregularly irregular. The abdomen was soft and non-tender without hepatosplenomegaly. No peripheral edema was present. Neurological exam showed intact cranial nerves and normal gait.
Current medication: allopurinol 100 mg 1-0-0, calcium carbonate 500 mg 1-0-0, cotrimoxazole 480 mg 1-0-0 (Mon/Wed/Fri), tamsulosin 0.4 mg 0-0-1, ramipril 5 mg 1-0-0, torasemide 10 mg 0-1-0, valaciclovir 500 mg 1-0-0, ophthalmic timolol 0.5% (1 drop both eyes BID), atorvastatin 10 mg 0-0-1, apixaban 2.5 mg 1-0-1, bisoprolol 2.5 mg 1-0-1, and pregabalin 75 mg 1-0-1.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "complete blood count"}}

Cbc Coagulation:
CBC: Hb 10.4 g/dL, WBC 6.1 × 10⁹/L, Plt 104 × 10⁹/L; MCV 90 fL, MCH 29 pg. Differential: neutrophils 60 %, lymphocytes 28 %, monocytes 8 %, eosinophils 3 %, basophils 1 %.
Coagulation: aPTT 31 s, INR 1.6.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "comprehensive metabolic panel"}}

Chemistry Immunology:
Chemistry: Na 134 mmol/L, K 5.1 mmol/L, creatinine 2.3 mg/dL (eGFR 26 mL/min/1.73 m²), BUN 48 mg/dL, AST 28 U/L, ALT 32 U/L, bilirubin 0.9 mg/dL, LDH 265 U/L, uric acid 8.2 mg/dL, Ca²⁺ 3.02 mmol/L, phosphate 4.9 mg/dL.
Immunology: IgG 1820 mg/dL, free κ light chains 412 mg/L, κ/λ ratio 48.
Urinalysis: protein +++ without hematuria.
NT-proBNP 2350 pg/mL.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "right shoulder x-ray"}}

Ct Skeleton:
2025-07-03: CT skeleton showed multiple osteolytic lesions of the axial and appendicular skeleton, most prominent in vertebrae, ribs, and pelvis, without signs of new destruction, consistent with inactive post-treatment myeloma lesions. Soft-tissue swelling at the right shoulder and proximal arm incompletely characterized due to lack of contrast.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Relapsed multiple myeloma with hypercalcemia and possible plasmacytoma of the right shoulder", "differentials": ["Extramedullary plasmacytoma", "Amyloidosis with soft tissue involvement", "Pathological fracture with hematoma"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Aggressive intravenous hydration and bisphosphonate therapy for hypercalcemia", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Serum protein electrophoresis with immunofixation and serum free light chain assay for disease quantification", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "MRI of the right shoulder to characterize the soft tissue mass and assess for plasmacytoma or pathological fracture", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Evaluation for re-initiation of anti-myeloma therapy (proteasome inhibitor and immunomodulatory agent-based regimen)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Nephrology consultation for management of acute kidney injury in the context of myeloma-related renal disease", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Relapsed multiple myeloma with hypercalcemia and possible plasmacytoma of the right shoulder

treatment_plan.action: Aggressive intravenous hydration and bisphosphonate therapy for hypercalcemia
Serum protein electrophoresis with immunofixation and serum free light chain assay for disease quantification
MRI of the right shoulder to characterize the soft tissue mass and assess for plasmacytoma or pathological fracture
Evaluation for re-initiation of anti-myeloma therapy (proteasome inhibitor and immunomodulatory agent-based regimen)
Nephrology consultation for management of acute kidney injury in the context of myeloma-related renal disease

content: {"evaluation": {"working_diagnosis": "correct", "differentials": ["correct", "partially_correct", "partially_correct"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "correct"}, {"action": "partially_correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "partially_correct"}, {"action": "partially_correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "partially_correct"}, {"action": "incorrect", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "incorrect"}, {"action": "incorrect", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "incorrect"}]}}

Round 2 begins.
Setting: Inpatient or day-hospital hematology workup
Capabilities: The patient is undergoing further diagnostic clarification. You can organize bone marrow and tissue sampling, detailed histopathology and immunophenotyping, comprehensive staging imaging, and extended laboratory evaluations. Interdisciplinary consultations are available. Some specialized test results may still be pending.
Request additional information or solve.

treatment_plan.action: correct
partially_correct
partially_correct
incorrect
incorrect

content: {"response": {"action": "request", "request": "serum protein electrophoresis with immunofixation"}}

Protein Studies:
Urinalysis: proteinuria (++), leukocyturia (120/µL), positive for albumin; κ/λ free light-chain ratio 18.3.
Serum protein electrophoresis revealed a distinct monoclonal IgG κ band with an M-spike, confirmed by immunofixation. Urine electrophoresis demonstrated monoclonal κ light-chain proteinuria.
β₂-microglobulin 4.8 mg/L, serum albumin 3.2 g/dL.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "MRI of right shoulder"}}

'MRI of right shoulder' not available. Try 'ct_skeleton' instead.
This case includes skeletal imaging (CT of the skeleton) rather than an MRI of the shoulder. If you want imaging of the involved bones/lesions, request the CT skeleton report.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "bone marrow biopsy"}}

Bone Marrow:
Bone marrow smears were normocellular with approximately 5-10 % plasma cells. The plasma cells appeared large with eccentric nuclei, coarse “clock-face” chromatin, prominent nucleoli, and abundant basophilic cytoplasm. Occasional binucleated forms were noted. The background marrow showed mildly reduced granulopoiesis and dysmorphic erythropoiesis, with scattered small lymphocytes and occasional mast cells. No excess of blasts was identified. Flow cytometry revealed 0.9 % plasma cells with strong expression of CD38⁺, CD138⁺, and CD27⁺ and dim CD56 expression, showing cytoplasmic kappa light-chain restriction. CD19 and CD45 were negative. No aberrant myeloid or B-cell populations were detected, 1.2% CD34low blasts.
Histology (core biopsy) demonstrated age-adjusted normocellular marrow (overall cellularity 40 %) with nodular and interstitial plasma-cell infiltrates, accounting for 5-10 % of total marrow cellularity. Immunohistochemistry confirmed CD138⁺ plasma cells with kappa light-chain restriction, negative for lambda. Residual trilineage hematopoiesis was present but mildly reduced with dysplastic erythropoiesis.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "tissue biopsy of right shoulder mass"}}

response.request: tissue biopsy of right shoulder mass

content:
'tissue biopsy of right shoulder mass' not available. Try 'ct_skeleton' instead.
This case does not include a pathology report of a soft-tissue biopsy. You could request imaging of the lesion (e.g., CT skeleton or PET-CT) or, if you meant bone marrow sampling, request the bone marrow report.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "PET-CT scan"}}

Pet Ct:
2025-07-06: FDG PET-CT: Demonstration of a focal FDG-avid lesion at the right shoulder and upper arm (SUVmax 8.2), correlating with the clinical swelling. Additional hypermetabolic foci were seen in the proximal humerus (SUVmax 6.5) and right scapula (SUVmax 5.9), compatible with active myeloma involvement. No other FDG-avid lesions were detected in the skeleton, visceral organs, or lymph nodes.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Relapsed IgG kappa multiple myeloma with plasmacytoma of the right shoulder, hypercalcemia, and renal impairment", "differentials": ["Extramedullary plasmacytoma with bone involvement", "Amyloid light-chain (AL) amyloidosis", "Metastatic carcinoma with lytic bone lesions"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Urgent intravenous hydration followed by zoledronic acid (dose-adjusted for renal function) for hypercalcemia management", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Initiation of daratumumab plus bortezomib and dexamethasone (DVd) regimen for relapsed myeloma", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Local radiation therapy to the right shoulder plasmacytoma for pain control and tumor reduction", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Renal consultation for management of myeloma-related kidney injury and optimization of renal function prior to chemotherapy", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 3}, {"action": "Continue infection prophylaxis with cotrimoxazole and monitor for tumor lysis syndrome during treatment initiation", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Relapsed IgG kappa multiple myeloma with plasmacytoma of the right shoulder, hypercalcemia, and renal impairment
differentials: Metastatic carcinoma with lytic bone lesions

treatment_plan.action: Urgent intravenous hydration followed by zoledronic acid (dose-adjusted for renal function) for hypercalcemia management
Initiation of daratumumab plus bortezomib and dexamethasone (DVd) regimen for relapsed myeloma
Local radiation therapy to the right shoulder plasmacytoma for pain control and tumor reduction
Renal consultation for management of myeloma-related kidney injury and optimization of renal function prior to chemotherapy
Continue infection prophylaxis with cotrimoxazole and monitor for tumor lysis syndrome during treatment initiation

Round 3 begins.
Setting: Specialist hematology conference (definitive diagnosis and treatment planning)
Capabilities: You now have access to definitive pathology, cytogenetics, molecular findings, full staging, and a detailed treatment history. You are expected to integrate these data with comorbidity and transplant scores to establish the final diagnosis, risk stratification, and a realistic treatment and follow-up strategy.
Request additional information or solve.
Final round - provide definitive diagnosis and treatment plan.

content: {"response": {"action": "request", "request": "treatment history and FISH cytogenetics"}}

Treatment History:
2013-03-01: Initial diagnosis of IgG κ multiple myeloma, without CRAB criteria. Staging: ISS II, Salmon & Durie II. Initial management: Watch & wait.
2014-09-01; Disease progression with anemia. Initiation of induction therapy with VCD for 3 cycles (q21d): Bortezomib 1.3 mg/m² s.c. (days 1, 4, 8, 11) , Cyclophosphamide 900 mg/m² i.v. (day 1), Dexamethasone 40 mg p.o. (days 1–4 and 9–12)
2015-09-01: Stem cell mobilization with CE regimen: Cyclophosphamide 2 g/m² i.v. (days 1–2), etoposide 100 mg/m² i.v. (days 1–3), followed by G-CSF 5 µg/kg/day from day 4 until stem cell collection.
2015-10-01: High-dose melphalan therapy 2 x 70 mg/m² i.v. (total 140 mg/m²), followed by autologous stem cell transplantation (ASCT). No maintenance therapy administered.
2017-01-01 Biochemical signs of relapse with slowly rising IgG (from 1300 → 1850 mg/dL over 12 months) and increasing κ free light chains (120 → 220 mg/L).
2018-02-04: Bone marrow biopsy: 20 % plasma cell infiltration. FISH: trisomy 3, t(4;14), 13q14 deletion, 1q21 gain, CDKN2A deletion. CT skeleton: no new osteolytic lesions.
2018-11-01: Slim-CRAB criteria met due to FLC ratio >100 (IgG 2312 mg/dL).
Echocardiogram: preserved systolic function (LVEF 58%), no valvular abnormalities. Worsening kidney function (creatinine 1.6 mg/dL). Start of KRD (q28d): Carfilzomib 20 mg/m² i.v. (days 1–2, cycle 1), then 27 mg/m² twice weekly (days 1–2, 8–9, 15–16). Lenalidomide 15 mg p.o. (days 1–21) Dexamethasone 40 mg weekly
2019-02-01: Serological complete remission (CR) achieved. No bone marrow reassessment performed.
2019-06-01: Second high-dose therapy with melphalan 2 x 70 mg/m² i.v. followed by ASCT.
Course complicated by onset of peripheral neuropathy and severe infectious episode (neutropenic fever requiring i.v. antibiotics) as well as acute kidney failure.
2021-04-01: Biochemical relapse: Immunofixation positive again with slowly rising IgG (1200 → 1650 mg/dL) and κ free light chains (84 → 187 mg/L).
2022-02-04. Start of Isa-Pom-Dex regimen: Isatuximab 10 mg/kg i.v. weekly x4, then every 2 weeks, Pomalidomide 3 mg p.o. (days 1–21), Dexamethasone 40 mg weekly: achieved VGPR.
2022-11-01: Lymphocyte apheresis performed in preparation for CAR-T therapy.
2023-01-30: CAR-T cell therapy with idecabtagene vicleucel following lymphodepletion with: Fludarabine 30 mg/m² i.v. (days –5 to –3, 50% dose reduction due to CKD stage 3), Cyclophosphamide 500 mg/m² i.v. (days –5 to –3). Infusion on 2023-01-30. Achieved VGPR, followed by CR on 2023-03-24.
2023-07-01 Cytopenia developed without evidence of therapy-related MDS or myeloma relapse.
2024-02-01 CT scan: new osteolytic lesions. IgG 1920 mg/dL and κ free light chains 191 mg/L. Immunofixation positive. Echocardiogram: LVEF 45%. Initiated ixazomib 4 mg (days 1, 8, 15) + dexamethasone 20 mg weekly, omitting IMiD due to mild cytopenia and CKD.
2024-10-01: Progressive cytopenia. Bone marrow: dysplastic changes with erythroid and myeloid atypia, no excess blasts. TP53 mutation (high VAF) detected in CD138-negative fraction; karyotyping unsuccessful. Ixazomib monotherapy continued briefly, but paused in December 2024 resulting in increased blood counts.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Triple-class refractory IgG kappa multiple myeloma relapsed after CAR-T therapy (ide-cel), with high-risk cytogenetics [t(4;14), 1q21 gain, del(13q), del(CDKN2A)], TP53 mutation, plasmacytoma of the right shoulder, hypercalcemia, and renal impairment", "differentials": ["Therapy-related myelodysplastic syndrome (given TP53 mutation in CD138-negative fraction and dysplastic marrow changes)", "Extramedullary relapse of myeloma without marrow involvement", "Secondary malignancy with TP53 mutation"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Denosumab 120 mg subcutaneously for hypercalcemia (preferred over zoledronic acid due to renal impairment with eGFR 26 mL/min)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Palliative radiation therapy to right shoulder plasmacytoma (20-30 Gy in 5-10 fractions) for pain control and local disease control", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Initiation of teclistamab (BCMAxCD3 bispecific antibody) for triple-class refractory myeloma post-CAR-T failure", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Referral for clinical trial enrollment for TP53-mutated, post-CAR-T relapsed myeloma (e.g., novel bispecifics, CELMoD, or cellular therapies)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Close monitoring for cytokine release syndrome and neurotoxicity during teclistamab initiation, with step-up dosing protocol", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 3}, {"action": "Repeat bone marrow biopsy with focused assessment for therapy-related MDS/AML given TP53 mutation in CD138-negative fraction and persistent cytopenias", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Triple-class refractory IgG kappa multiple myeloma relapsed after CAR-T therapy (ide-cel), with high-risk cytogenetics [t(4;14), 1q21 gain, del(13q), del(CDKN2A)], TP53 mutation, plasmacytoma of the right shoulder, hypercalcemia, and renal impairment
differentials: Therapy-related myelodysplastic syndrome (given TP53 mutation in CD138-negative fraction and dysplastic marrow changes)
Extramedullary relapse of myeloma without marrow involvement

treatment_plan.action: Denosumab 120 mg subcutaneously for hypercalcemia (preferred over zoledronic acid due to renal impairment with eGFR 26 mL/min)
Palliative radiation therapy to right shoulder plasmacytoma (20-30 Gy in 5-10 fractions) for pain control and local disease control
Initiation of teclistamab (BCMAxCD3 bispecific antibody) for triple-class refractory myeloma post-CAR-T failure
Referral for clinical trial enrollment for TP53-mutated, post-CAR-T relapsed myeloma (e.g., novel bispecifics, CELMoD, or cellular therapies)
Close monitoring for cytokine release syndrome and neurotoxicity during teclistamab initiation, with step-up dosing protocol
Repeat bone marrow biopsy with focused assessment for therapy-related MDS/AML given TP53 mutation in CD138-negative fraction and persistent cytopenias
